ip_outcome_note: Card title is 'Gideon's Sacrifice' and is marked story_spotlight:true on Scryfall. The card depicts two figures: one engulfed in red magical explosion (Gideon Jura absorbing the contract-breaking death-curse) and one in purple robes recoiling in shock (Liliana Vess). The flavor text — 'To Liliana's surprise, the cost of betraying Bolas did not fall on her' — explicitly names Liliana and describes the WAR climax moment. Both suspected IP figures (Gideon Jura and Liliana Vess) are Wizards-internal MTG characters. Verified.

trivia_bullets.text: The flavor text reads: 'To Liliana's surprise, the cost of betraying Bolas did not fall on her.' This line names both key figures directly and encapsulates the War of the Spark climax: Liliana broke her demonic contract with Nicol Bolas by turning his Dreadhorde armies against him, which should have killed her — but Gideon stepped in and transferred the contract's death-curse onto himself, dying in her place.

Gideon's Sacrifice is marked as a Story Spotlight card in War of the Spark (released May 3, 2019), a designation Wizards uses for cards that depict pivotal moments in the set's narrative. War of the Spark was the culmination of a multi-set arc involving Nicol Bolas's plan to harvest planeswalker sparks using the Immortal Sun on Ravnica. Gideon Jura's death is the emotional climax of that story.
sources: Wargamer: War of the Spark set guide

trivia_bullets.text: The card is illustrated by Chris Rallis (also credited on Scryfall API) and is a {W} instant — mechanically, the effect maps directly onto the story moment: redirect all damage dealt to you and your permanents onto one chosen permanent, exactly as Gideon took the contract's killing blow onto himself. The card's mechanics are unusually literal for a flavor-mechanic translation.

Gideon's Sacrifice is a Common with EDHREC rank 5679, indicating modest but real Commander play. It sees more play in Pauper and Pioneer formats than in Commander. The card received a reprint in the Murders at Karlov Manor Commander decks (MKC no. 68, 2024), suggesting continued functional relevance in white's damage-redirect toolkit.

Gideon Jura (Kytheon Iora of Akros) is one of the original five Gatewatch members. His indestructibility hieromancy — which he passed to Liliana in the story — is reflected across multiple card printings throughout his arc. Liliana Vess is among the longest-running planeswalker characters in MTG, first appearing in Lorwyn (2007). Their dynamic in WAR is a rare instance of an antagonist-turned-reluctant-ally moment anchored by a supporting character's death.
sources: Hipsters of the Coast: Gideon, Liliana and War of the Spark
DailyEsports: WAR Story Recap Act III
